subject: [PATCH v5 0/2] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix PAGEMAP_SCAN written state for PMD holes

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David suggested reusing code from unpopulated_scan_test() in unpopulated_thp_scan_test(), proposing either adding a 'use_thp' parameter or factoring out common functionality into a helper function.
reviews.raw_body:
Sorry if I wasn't clear. I think unpopulated_thp_scan_test() can actually reuse
bits of unpopulated_scan_test().

Either by having a magical "use_thp" parameter or by factoring stuff out into a
helper.

Or am I missing an important point why that would not work or be really ugly?

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to David's review by agreeing that the patch looks good and making no changes or promises for further revision.

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the PAGEMAP_SCAN functionality, which incorrectly reports PMD holes as not-written in uffd-wp VMAs. The fix involves updating the code to correctly report the written state of PMD holes. A selftest is also added to verify the correctness of the fix.
